title: Multi-error with chain that handles 4 distinct error types
prompt: Refactor the nested case chain in test_fixtures/nested_case_auth.ex into lib/my_app/auth/sign_in.ex. The flow: (1) look up user by email (returns {:ok, user} | {:error, :not_found}), (2) verify password (returns :ok | {:error, :invalid_password}), (3) check account is active (returns :ok | {:error, :locked}), (4) issue session token (returns {:ok, token} | {:error, :token_gen_failed}). Use a single `with` expression that chains all four steps. The `else` clause must pattern-match on EACH distinct error tuple and return {:error, :user_not_found | :invalid_credentials | :account_locked | :session_failed} respectively (note the DIFFERENT public-facing error atoms). Do NOT catch a generic {:error, _} fallthrough. The happy path returns {:ok, %{user: user, token: token}}.
tier_rationale: Requires the disciplined with-else pattern with 4 distinct error arms, NOT a generic fallthrough. The atom-translation (internal error → public error) is the subtle part Sonnet often flattens into a single clause.
research_citation: Dashbit: 'with expressions + pattern-matched else clauses are the canonical Elixir error-handling pattern'
